extraction.title: Uncertainty-Aware Hybrid Retrieval for Long-Document RAG

sources.domain: Experiments on question answering benchmarks show that uncertainty-aware fusion and parent promotion improve generation quality while maintaining a lightweight, plug-and-play retrieval pipeline.
sources.hybrid_type: We propose Uncertainty-aware Multi-Granularity RAG (UMG-RAG), a training-free hybrid retrieval framework that treats chunk granularity as query-specific reliability estimation.

extraction.title: Multi-Field Hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Maritime Accident Root Cause Analysis

sources.domain: Maritime accident adjudication reports contain critical tribunal findings for root cause analysis (RCA), yet retrieving relevant precedents and drafting consistent reports from decades of records remains labor-intensive. This paper proposes a multi-field hybrid retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) framework for automated maritime RCA
sources.hybrid_type: Our retrieval strategy employs a field-aware hybrid approach, fusing sparse and dense rankings via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).
sources.llm_used: Furthermore, grounding the generator on the retrieved precedents enhances RCA generation quality over an LLM-only baseline

extraction.title: A Hybrid Retrieval and Reranking Framework for Evidence-Grounded Retrieval-Augmented Generation

fields.domain: biomedical and healthcare-related document question answering

sources.domain: This study presents a hybrid retrieval and reranking framework for citation-aware RAG in biomedical and healthcare-related document question answering.
sources.hybrid_type: Hybrid retrieval first retrieves candidate evidence chunks, and Cohere reranking then prioritizes the most relevant passages before answer generation.
sources.vector_db: Source PDF documents are stored in Amazon S3, embedded using Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2, and indexed with Amazon OpenSearch Serverless.
sources.llm_used: The framework uses Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases for document ingestion, parsing, chunking, embedding generation, and evidence retrieval.
sources.embedding_model: Source PDF documents are stored in Amazon S3, embedded using Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2, and indexed with Amazon OpenSearch Serverless.

extraction.title: Agentic Hybrid RAG for Evidence-Grounded Muon Collider Analysis

fields.hybrid_type: hybrid retriever, integrating sparse lexical and dense semantic retrieval

sources.domain: Muon collider research spans accelerator physics, detector instrumentation, and high-energy phenomenology
sources.hybrid_type: The framework combines a hybrid retriever, integrating sparse lexical and dense semantic retrieval, with an agentic reasoning module

extraction.title: Efficient RAG with Intent-Aware Retrieval and Semantics-Preserving Chunking

sources.domain: Our method achieves significant gains on multi-hop and evidence-sensitive tasks, with a 2.65-point improvement in F1 on HotPotQA and a 1.5-point increase in accuracy on FEVER.
sources.hybrid_type: IAR implements a dynamic hybrid retrieval method that adaptively weights the retrieval channels based on the query intent
sources.llm_used: To alleviate the computational latency brought by our iterative mechanism, we leverage small language models (SLMs).

extraction.title: Hierarchical Abstract Tree for Cross-Document Retrieval-Augmented Generation

sources.domain: On cross-document multi-hop QA benchmarks

extraction.title: AR-RAG: Autoregressive Retrieval Augmentation for Image Generation

sources.domain: AR-RAG: Autoregressive Retrieval Augmentation for Image Generation
sources.hybrid_type: We introduce Autoregressive Retrieval Augmentation (AR-RAG), a novel paradigm that enhances image generation by autoregressively incorporating k nearest neighbor retrievals at the patch level.

extraction.title: EVOR: Evolving Retrieval for Code Generation

sources.domain: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has been successfully applied in code generation.
sources.hybrid_type: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has been successfully applied in code generation.
sources.llm_used: limiting the adaptation capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to domains they have insufficient knowledge of.

extraction.title: Automated Literature Review Using NLP Techniques and LLM-Based Retrieval-Augmented Generation

sources.domain: Automated Literature Review Using NLP Techniques and LLM-Based Retrieval-Augmented Generation This research presents and compares multiple approaches to automate the generation of literature reviews
sources.hybrid_type: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with a Large Language Model (LLM)
sources.llm_used: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with Large Language Model (GPT-3.5-turbo)

extraction.title: MUST-RAG: MUSical Text Question Answering with Retrieval Augmented Generation

sources.domain: to adapt general-purpose LLMs for text-only music question answering (MQA) tasks.
sources.hybrid_type: we propose MusT-RAG, a comprehensive framework based on Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to adapt general-purpose LLMs for text-only music question answering (MQA) tasks.
sources.vector_db: we (1) propose MusWikiDB, a music-specialized vector database for the retrieval stage

extraction.title: LURE-RAG: Lightweight Utility-driven Reranking for Efficient RAG

sources.domain: downstream task such as question answering
sources.hybrid_type: Lightweight Utility-driven Reranking for Efficient RAG (LURE-RAG), a framework that augments any black-box retriever with an efficient LambdaMART-based reranker.

extraction.title: Efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Token Co-occurrence Graphs

sources.domain: We evaluated TIGRAG on three widely adopted multi-hop Question Answering (QA) benchmarks.
sources.llm_used: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates hallucinations in Large Language Models (LLMs)

extraction.title: RMIT-ADM+S at the MMU-RAG NeurIPS 2025 Competition

sources.domain: This paper presents the award-winning RMIT-ADM+S system for the Text-to-Text track of the NeurIPS~2025 MMU-RAG Competition.
sources.hybrid_type: We introduce Routing-to-RAG (R2RAG), a research-focused retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architecture composed of lightweight components that dynamically adapt the retrieval strategy based on inferred query complexity and evidence sufficiency.

extraction.title: Simple Is Effective: The Roles of Graphs and Large Language Models in Knowledge-Graph-Based Retrieval-Augmented Generation

sources.domain: Knowledge Graph (KG)-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses these issues by grounding LLM outputs in structured external knowledge from KGs.
sources.hybrid_type: We introduce SubgraphRAG, extending the KG-based RAG framework that retrieves subgraphs and leverages LLMs for reasoning and answer prediction.
sources.llm_used: Notably, based on our retrieved subgraphs, smaller LLMs like Llama3.1-8B-Instruct deliver competitive results with explainable reasoning, while larger models like GPT-4o achieve state-of-the-art accuracy compared with previous baselines

extraction.title: Dissecting Agentic RAG: A Component Ablation for Multi-Hop QA with a Local 7B Model

sources.domain: Agentic retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems combine iterative reasoning loops, query decomposition, and adaptive retrieval to tackle multi-hop question answering.
sources.hybrid_type: (1) fixed hybrid retrieval via reciprocal rank fusion consistently outperforms rule-based adaptive routing

extraction.title: Covering the Unseen: Information Demand Coverage Optimization for Retrieval-Augmented Generation

sources.domain: On six open-domain QA benchmarks, GeoRAG improves exact match (EM) by +6.5 to +7.5 points over top-k truncation (up to +9.7 on HotpotQA and ASQA) and outperforms strong baselines including MMR, DPP, BGE-Reranker, SMART-RAG, and AdaGReS, with stable gains across context budgets and sub-query generators.
sources.hybrid_type: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) typically treats context selection as ranking chunks against a single query embedding.

extraction.title: AGE: Adaptive-masking for Graph Embedding in Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation

sources.domain: Our experimental results indicate that AGE significantly improves approaches using non-parametric search component in GraphQA tasks
sources.llm_used: supports large language models (LLMs) by referring to graph-structured data as external knowledge. While this technique ideally captures intricate relationships, it often struggles with graph representations for LLMs, particularly for frozen LLMs
sources.embedding_model: AGE employs a Transformer in a mask-based self-supervised learning (SSL) approach. We designed the architecture similar to text embedding encoders

extraction.title: Towards More Robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Evaluating RAG Under Adversarial Poisoning Attacks

sources.domain: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems have emerged as a promising solution to mitigate LLM hallucinations and enhance their performance in knowledge-intensive domains.

synthesis.research_question: What hybrid retrieval strategies and components (dense, sparse, reranking, graph) do the included systems use?
synthesis.narrative:
The included systems employ a variety of hybrid retrieval strategies and components. Some systems combine dense and sparse retrieval methods, often using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to fuse their rankings [P002, P019]. For instance, one framework uses Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2 for embedding and Amazon OpenSearch Serverless for indexing, combining candidate retrieval with reranking by Cohere [P003]. Another approach, Uncertainty-aware Multi-Granularity RAG (UMG-RAG), uses existing dense and sparse retrievers as complementary experts across multiple chunk granularities, fusing candidates based on query-specific confidence [P001].

Reranking is a common component, with some systems using cross-encoders for reranking [P019] or a LambdaMART-based reranker trained with an LLM utility-guided listwise ranking loss [P014]. One framework integrates a hybrid retriever, combining sparse lexical and dense semantic retrieval, with an agentic reasoning module for query decomposition and evidence expansion [P004]. Another system uses an agent-powered hybrid retriever as part of a multi-granular retrieval agent [P006].

Graph-based components are also utilized. One framework, TIGRAG, uses a token co-occurrence knowledge graph and combines graph-based semantic expansion with neural reranking for multi-hop reasoning [P016]. Other systems leverage knowledge graphs for retrieval, with one approach, SubgraphRAG, retrieving subgraphs and using a multilayer perceptron with a parallel triple-scoring mechanism for efficient subgraph retrieval [P018]. Adaptive masking for graph embedding (AGE) is employed to address misalignment between graph-based and text-based latent features in GraphRAG [P021]. PathRouter is a path-aware training framework for agentic GraphRAG that jointly evaluates trajectories based on answer correctness and evidence-path overlap [P022].

Some systems incorporate adaptive or dynamic retrieval strategies. One framework uses an intention-aware retriever that adaptively weights retrieval channels based on query intent [P005]. Another system, Routing-to-RAG (R2RAG), dynamically adapts the retrieval strategy based on inferred query complexity and evidence sufficiency [P017].
